title_zh: NVIDIA Vera Rubin实现后训练工作负载的每美元最大智能——代理AI的关键指标
title_en: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
summary_zh: 借助极致协同设计，NVIDIA Vera Rubin实现了最低的每令牌成本，在代理时代显著提升后训练的每美元智能度。
summary_en: Through extreme codesign, NVIDIA Vera Rubin achieves the lowest cost per token, maximizing intelligence per dollar for post-training workloads in the agentic era.
why_it_matters_zh: 在代理AI时代，提升后训练的成本效益至关重要，NVIDIA Vera Rubin通过降低每令牌成本，显著提高了投资回报，推动智能应用发展。
why_it_matters_en: Maximizing cost efficiency in post-training is critical for agentic AI; NVIDIA Vera Rubin significantly boosts return on investment by lowering cost per token, advancing intelligent applications.

title_zh: 腾讯更新 Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0 于 Hugging Face
title_en: Tencent Updates Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0 on Hugging Face
summary_zh: 腾讯在 Hugging Face 上更新了 Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0，支持 safetensors、统一多目标跟踪（unified_mot）、任意风格转换（any-to-any）、混元系列（hunyuan）、视觉-语言交互等功能，标签涵盖图像文本生成。该模型下载量达159次，获得38个点赞。
summary_en: Tencent has updated Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0 on Hugging Face, featuring safetensors format, unified multi-object tracking, any-to-any style conversion, Hunyuan series capabilities, and vision-language interaction. Tagged for image-text-to-text tasks, the model has 159 downloads and 38 likes.
why_it_matters_zh: 该模型融合了视觉与语言多模态技术，支持多目标跟踪和任意风格转换，提升了图像与文本的交互能力，适用范围广泛。
why_it_matters_en: This model integrates vision and language multimodal techniques, supporting multi-object tracking and any-to-any style conversion, enhancing image-text interaction with wide applicability.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Tencent Updates Hy-Embodied-RxBrain-1.0 on Hugging Face. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 用于自主无人机群搜救的智能三级学习架构
title_en: Intelligent Three Level Learning Architecture for Autonomous UAV Swarms in Search and Rescue
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种新颖的三级层次学习架构，适用于执行搜救任务的自主无人机群。该架构结合了三种不同的学习机制，分别对应生物学中的反射、技能和推理层次，包含Hebbian神经可塑性、多智能体强化学习结合图神经网络与行为树，以及基于BDI推理和数字孪生的模型无关元学习。架构通过22个建筑契约保障安全性、预算正确性、最优性、活性等六类形式保证，并引入群体元认知，实现群体自我监控和策略切换。理论分析表明，该架构克服了现有层次强化学习方法的五个根本限制。
summary_en: This paper introduces a novel three-level hierarchical learning architecture for autonomous UAV swarms in search and rescue missions. It integrates three distinct learning mechanisms reflecting biological reflexes, skills, and reasoning: Hebbian neuroplasticity for individual adaptation, multi-agent reinforcement learning with graph neural networks and behavior trees for tactical coordination, and model-agnostic meta learning with BDI reasoning and digital twins for strategic decisions. The design is formalized with twenty-two architectural contracts ensuring six classes of formal guarantees like safety, optimality, and consistency. The concept of swarm meta cognition enables the swarm to monitor and switch cognitive strategies. Theoretical analysis demonstrates the architecture overcomes five fundamental limitations of existing hierarchical reinforcement learning approaches.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究创新地融合了多种生物启发的学习机制，形成层次分明且相互协作的架构，显著提升无人机群在复杂搜救任务中的自主性和鲁棒性。通过形式化契约保障多种关键性能指标，确保系统安全且高效运行，具备较强的理论与应用价值。
why_it_matters_en: This work innovatively combines several biologically-inspired learning mechanisms into a cohesive hierarchical architecture, enhancing autonomy and robustness for UAV swarms in complex search and rescue tasks. The formalized contracts guarantee critical properties like safety and optimality, ensuring reliable and efficient operation, which brings substantial theoretical and practical significance.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Intelligent Three Level Learning Architecture for Autonomous UAV Swarms in Search and Rescue. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 69.

title_zh: HG-RAG：用于结构化知识图谱的层级引导检索增强生成
title_en: HG-RAG: Hierarchy-Guided Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Structured Knowledge Graphs
summary_zh: 本文提出了HG-RAG，一种基于层级知识图谱图遍历的检索增强生成框架。该方法通过解析查询中的命名实体锚点，向上扩展至父节点，横向扩展至关联邻居节点，并在需要时向下扩展至子节点，从而为语言模型提供结构化上下文。实验证明，HG-RAG在处理层级、关系和多跳推理任务时，性能优于传统的扁平文档检索方法，同时有效减少了生成中的幻觉并保持上下文连贯性。
summary_en: This paper introduces HG-RAG, a retrieval-augmented generation framework that leverages graph traversal over hierarchical knowledge graphs to supply structured context to language models. The retrieval pipeline anchors named entities from queries and expands context upward to parent nodes, laterally to relational neighbors, and downward to child nodes as needed. Evaluation on different scales and query types shows HG-RAG outperforms flat document retrieval baselines in hierarchical, relational, and multi-hop reasoning tasks, while reducing hallucinations and maintaining local coherence.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统的RAG系统在处理结构化知识的层级和关系推理时表现不足，HG-RAG通过引入层级图遍历机制，有效提升了检索上下文的结构化和相关性，改善了推理能力，减少了模型生成的错误信息。
why_it_matters_en: Conventional RAG systems struggle with hierarchical and relational reasoning over structured knowledge. HG-RAG addresses this by incorporating hierarchy-guided graph traversal to retrieve more structured and relevant context, enhancing reasoning performance and reducing hallucinations in generated outputs.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: HG-RAG: Hierarchy-Guided Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Structured Knowledge Graphs. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 70.

title_zh: 基于交互作用的模型解释（IMEX）
summary_zh: 在预测建模中，解释模型为何给出特定预测结果变得越来越重要。IMEX方法是一种可解释预测建模方向，旨在识别对目标预测贡献最大的变量及其交互作用，支持高阶交互分析。IMEX包含静态相关力（PCS）和交互相关力（PCI）两项指标，分别衡量单变量贡献和变量间非加性效应。本文通过与INVASE对比，在三个人工数据集上验证了PCS，表明IMEX能够在非线性、条件及多重共线性关系下准确恢复特征结构。
summary_en: Explaining why a model produces a given target prediction is increasingly important in predictive modeling. The IMEX approach is a methodological direction in explainable predictive modeling, designed to identify variables and their interactions contributing most to the prediction, without limiting higher-order interactions. IMEX utilizes two metrics: Static Correlation Power (PCS) for individual feature contribution and Interaction Correlation Power (PCI) for non-additive effects. Experimental validation comparing PCS with INVASE on synthetic datasets shows IMEX can recover relevant feature structures under complex relationships.
why_it_matters_zh: IMEX提供了一种系统方法识别关键特征及其交互作用，提升黑箱模型的可解释性，对于涉及复杂变量关系的预测模型尤为重要，有助于模型的信任与验证。
why_it_matters_en: IMEX offers a systematic way to identify key features and their interactions, enhancing interpretability of black-box models. This is crucial for predictive models with complex variable relationships, aiding trust and validation.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: IMEX Interaction-Based Model Explanation. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 62.

summary_zh: 此次更新修复了单段目录规则可自动批准对任意树内嵌套目录写入的问题，修复了Windows PowerShell 5.1会话中的权限检查绕过漏洞，以及修正了Bash中文件描述符重定向形式权限检查未正确失败的问题。
summary_en: This update fixed an issue where single-segment directory/allow rules like Edit(src/) auto-approved writes to nested dir/ directories anywhere in the tree, fixed a permission-check bypass affecting commands run in Windows PowerShell 5.1 sessions, and corrected Bash permission checks to fail properly on file-descriptor redirect forms.

why_it_matters_en: These fixes improve the accuracy and security of permission management, preventing unauthorized file writes and command execution bypasses.

title_en: A Scorecard for the AI Age

title_zh: RegNetAgents：用于癌症基因组学中跨网络调控驱动因子识别的多智能体框架
title_en: RegNetAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Cross-Network Regulatory Driver Identification in Cancer Genomics
summary_zh: RegNetAgents 是一个面向 AI 的多智能体框架，用于在异构基因调控网络中结构化且基于查询的调控候选因子识别。该系统整合了来自 TCGA 的癌症网络和 GREmLN 项目的单细胞调控网络，实现了肿瘤块体和单细胞 ARACNe 网络的统一分析。针对特定基因，框架执行双网络分类、OncoKB 注释的癌症基因筛选及肿瘤衍生调控关系的作用模式分配。候选因子根据跨网络证据一致性进行排序。系统以多智能体 LangGraph DAG 工作流形式实现，通过 Python API 和 Model Context Protocol (MCP) 客户端访问，作为预计算调控网络上的下游分析层，而非网络推断工具。在十一种乳腺癌和十二种结直肠癌焦点基因分析中，RegNetAgents 识别的候选调控因子显著富集 OncoKB 注释的癌症基因，验证了其信号特异性。扩展模块支持肿瘤潜力、药物可及性、临床相关性和网络脆弱性的结构化评估，助力从候选识别到生物学假设生成的端到端解释。
summary_en: RegNetAgents is an AI-oriented multi-agent framework for structured, query-driven identification of regulatory candidate drivers across heterogeneous gene regulatory networks. It integrates TCGA-derived cancer networks with large-scale single-cell regulatory networks from the GREmLN project, enabling unified analysis of bulk tumor and single-cell ARACNe networks. For a given focal gene, it performs dual-network classification, cancer gene filtering using OncoKB annotations, and mode-of-action assignment for tumor-derived regulatory relationships, ranking candidates by evidence consistency across networks. Implemented as a LangGraph DAG workflow accessible via Python API and MCP client, it operates downstream of precomputed regulatory networks rather than as a network inference method. Applied to eleven breast cancer and twelve colorectal cancer focal genes, RegNetAgents identifies candidate regulators significantly enriched for OncoKB-annotated cancer genes, demonstrating specificity. An extended module allows structured evaluation of oncogenic potential, druggability, clinical relevance, and network vulnerability, supporting end-to-end interpretation from candidate identification to biological hypothesis generation.
why_it_matters_zh: RegNetAgents 为癌症基因组学中复杂的调控网络分析提供了创新且可解释的多智能体 AI 框架，能够整合多源异构数据，精准识别癌症驱动调控因子，促进癌症机制研究和临床转化。
why_it_matters_en: RegNetAgents offers an innovative and interpretable multi-agent AI framework for complex regulatory network analysis in cancer genomics, enabling integration of heterogeneous data sources to accurately identify cancer driver regulators, thereby advancing cancer biology insights and potential clinical applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: RegNetAgents: A Multi-Agent Framework for Cross-Network Regulatory Driver Identification in Cancer Genomics. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: DialogueVPR：迈向对话式视觉地点识别
title_en: DialogueVPR: Towards Conversational Visual Place Recognition
summary_zh: Inspired by how humans communicate spatial information, DialogueVPR提出了一种基于对话的视觉地点识别新范式。该方法通过交互式对话推理过程处理语言描述中的歧义与不完整性，显著提升了地点定位的准确性。作者发布了首个对话式地点识别大规模基准DlgQuest-Cities，并设计一个联合多模态检索与智能提问的统一推理框架DQ-pilot，通过分阶段训练和强化学习优化检索性能。实验证明该方法在地点识别任务中明显优于传统静态检索基线。代码和模型已开源。
summary_en: Inspired by how humans communicate spatial information, DialogueVPR introduces a new conversational paradigm for visual place recognition. It models localization as an interactive dialogue-driven reasoning process to handle ambiguity and incompleteness in natural language descriptions, significantly improving accuracy. The authors provide DlgQuest-Cities, the first large-scale dialogue-based benchmark, and propose a unified framework coupling a cross-modal retriever with an intelligent questioner DQ-pilot, trained via supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning. Experiments demonstrate the reasoning-based approach substantially outperforms traditional static retrieval baselines. Code and model are publicly available.
why_it_matters_zh: 当前静态、一次性检索方法难以解决自然语言描述中的不确定性和信息缺失。DialogueVPR通过引入对话机制，实现动态交互推理，为视觉地点识别带来了更强的灵活性和准确性，推动了语言引导的地理定位技术发展。
why_it_matters_en: Static one-shot retrieval methods struggle with ambiguity and incomplete information in natural language descriptions. DialogueVPR addresses this by introducing a dialog-based interactive reasoning process, offering enhanced flexibility and accuracy in visual place recognition, thereby advancing language-guided geo-localization techniques.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: DialogueVPR: Towards Conversational Visual Place Recognition. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 73.

title_zh: 面向闭环1型糖尿病控制的可解释语言模型
title_en: Interpretable Language Model for Closed-Loop Type 1 Diabetes Control
summary_zh: 本文提出了LLM-T1D，一种结合强化学习（RL）精度与大型语言模型（LLM）人类化推理的胰岛素泵控制器。通过训练专家RL系统并蒸馏知识至微调后的LLaMA 3.1 8B和Qwen3 8B模型，开发出不仅超越RL性能且能用易懂语言解释决策的控制器。经FDA批准的UVA/Padova模拟器测试，LLM控制器实现了73.5%的血糖控制时间在目标范围内，并通过正式方法严格验证，保证对幻觉信息的安全性。
summary_en: This paper presents LLM-T1D, a method that integrates the precision of Reinforcement Learning (RL) with the clear, human-like reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) to design an insulin pump controller. By training an expert RL system and distilling its knowledge into fine-tuned LLaMA 3.1 8B and Qwen3 8B models, the resulting controller not only outperforms the RL system but also explains its decisions in plain language. Tested on the FDA-approved UVA/Padova simulator, the LLM controllers achieve 73.5% Time in Range blood sugar control while maintaining strict formal safety verification against hallucinations.
why_it_matters_zh: 由于1型糖尿病胰岛素治疗需要精确且安全的自动化控制，结合强化学习和大型语言模型的LLM-T1D提供了既高效又透明的解决方案，提高了患者和医生的信任度，有望推动临床应用。
why_it_matters_en: Managing Type 1 Diabetes requires precise and safe insulin delivery. LLM-T1D combines the strengths of reinforcement learning and large language models to create a transparent and reliable controller, enhancing trust for patients and clinicians and potentially accelerating clinical adoption.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Interpretable Language Model for Closed-Loop Type 1 Diabetes Control. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 66.

title_zh: 人工智能构建贝叶斯网络以支持运营决策——虚拟调查方法
title_en: Human AI Construction of Bayesian Networks for Operational Decision Support -- A Virtual Survey Approach
summary_zh: 贝叶斯信念网络是处理不确定性决策的有力工具，但其结构构建和参数估计均较为困难。该研究提出一种利用大型语言模型和一组具有特定角色与场景的AI代理共同估计概率的方法，融合专家意见与数据驱动学习。通过裁剪均值法去噪，构建出六步贝叶斯信念网络框架，并以替代医疗体系中的客户咨询意向为例。结果显示，自我效能感的因果影响较小，主观规范影响更大，最有效策略是同步提升信心与社区规范。
summary_en: Bayesian Belief Networks (BBNs) are effective for decision-making under uncertainty but are challenging to build and parameterize. This study introduces a methodology using large language models with a panel of AI agents simulating specific personas and contexts to estimate probabilities, bridging expert judgment and data-driven approaches. Noise is reduced via a trimmed-mean rule within a six-step BBN framework. Applied to modeling customer intention to consult a doctor in alternative healthcare, findings show that while self-efficacy seems important, its causal impact is small compared to stronger effects from subjective norms. The best strategy is enhancing both confidence and community norms.
why_it_matters_zh: 该方法创新性地结合了AI代理虚拟调查与统计去噪技术，解决传统专家意见与数据驱动学习的矛盾，提升了贝叶斯网络构建的效率和准确性，具有重要的理论和实际应用价值。
why_it_matters_en: This approach innovatively integrates AI agent-based virtual surveys with statistical denoising, bridging the gap between traditional expert judgment and data-driven learning. It improves Bayesian network construction efficiency and accuracy, offering significant theoretical and practical implications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Human AI Construction of Bayesian Networks for Operational Decision Support -- A Virtual Survey Approach. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 67.

title_zh: 来源于访问结构的能力，而非规模：混合序列模型的下界与预注册测试
title_en: Capability from Access Structure, Not Scale: Lower Bounds and Pre-Registered Tests for Hybrid Sequence Models
summary_zh: 本文提出能力收敛假说（Capability Convergence Hypothesis, CCH），指出在固定的每步推理预算下，表示收敛并不等同于能力收敛。真正的能力收敛趋向于一种访问完备混合架构，该架构结合了压缩的O(1)状态通道和可扩展的逐字索引通道。通过牛顿苹果问题等基准任务，本文揭示了三大资源壁垒，并通过信息论下界和预注册实验验证了该假说，同时区分了理论证明和经济学动机的猜想。首次小规模预注册测试支持了模型在访问结构影响下的能力提升，表现出超加性特点。
summary_en: The paper proposes the Capability Convergence Hypothesis (CCH), stating that under fixed per-token inference budget, representational convergence does not imply capability convergence. Instead, capability converges toward an access-complete hybrid architecture combining a compressive O(1)-state channel and a scalable verbatim-index channel. Using benchmark tasks like the Newton's apple problem, it identifies three fundamental resource walls and substantiates the hypothesis through information-theoretic lower bounds and pre-registered experiments. The first small-scale pre-registered tests confirm that capability improvements depend on access structure rather than scale, exhibiting strict super-additivity under composition.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究深入探讨了序列模型能力提升的本质，指出仅靠模型规模扩展无法保证能力全面进步，强调了访问结构设计的重要性。这对于理解大型混合序列模型的性能瓶颈和指导未来架构优化具有重要理论和实践价值。
why_it_matters_en: This research deeply examines the essence of capability improvements in sequence models, highlighting that scaling alone does not guarantee comprehensive ability gains, and emphasizing the critical role of access structure design. It holds significant theoretical and practical importance for understanding performance bottlenecks in large hybrid sequence models and guiding future architecture optimization.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Capability from Access Structure, Not Scale: Lower Bounds and Pre-Registered Tests for Hybrid Sequence Models. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.
ranking_reason: Selected from authoritative primary source; quality score 71.

title_zh: ToolAnchor：锚定反事实上下文以提升代理工具使用能力
title_en: ToolAnchor: Anchoring Counterfactual Context to Boost Agentic Tool-use Capability
summary_zh: ToolAnchor通过在关键决策点注入反事实锚定上下文，突破代理行为惯性，激发其利用新工具的能力，解决了工具集扩展时代理频繁依赖旧工具的难题。该框架利用教师模型假设反事实情境，由学生模型验证并通过后训练内化改进策略，在多项任务中展现出稳定的性能提升。
summary_en: ToolAnchor injects counterfactual anchor contexts at critical decision points to overcome behavioral inertia in agents, enabling effective use of new tools beyond their fixed training sets. Using teacher models to hypothesize contexts and student rollouts for validation, it internalizes successful strategies via post-training. Evaluations demonstrate consistent competitive performance across diverse tasks under expanded toolsets.
why_it_matters_zh: 传统工具增强代理在面对新工具时往往表现不佳，主要因行为惯性导致依赖既有工具。ToolAnchor通过反事实情境引导代理尝试被抑制的能力，有效提升了代理在动态工具扩展中的适应性和表现，促进了可扩展的代理强化学习发展。
why_it_matters_en: Conventional tool-augmented agents struggle to integrate new tools due to behavioral inertia preferring familiar ones. ToolAnchor uses counterfactual contexts to induce suppressed capabilities, enhancing adaptability and performance during dynamic toolset expansions, thereby advancing scalable agentic reinforcement learning.

title_zh: 通过知识图谱定位提升小型语言模型的推理能力
title_en: Enhancing Small Language Models Reasoning through Knowledge Graph Grounding
summary_zh: 尽管大型语言模型在零样本推理上表现出色，但其部署成本高且对环境影响大。小型语言模型（SLMs）作为可持续替代方案，在复杂多跳逻辑推理任务中易出错。本文提出一种神经符号代理框架，通过两种专用工具调用，利用关系图卷积网络（RGCN）为SLMs（如Gemma 3和Llama 3.2）提供知识图谱支持。实验基于CLUTRR亲属关系基准，在真实和Oracle场景下验证方法有效性。结果显示，RGCN提示相较故事文本基线性能提升1.5至2倍，但知识抽取瓶颈和多跳推理连锁错误限制了效果。此外，某些架构中自生成噪声事实导致“干扰效应”，削弱了专家提示的效果。该研究揭示了低资源神经符号系统中符号定位的挑战，并为迭代验证提供了路径。
summary_en: Large language models excel at zero-shot reasoning but are costly and environmentally demanding. Small Language Models (SLMs) offer a sustainable alternative but struggle with complex multi-hop logical tasks. This study introduces a neuro-symbolic agentic framework that enhances SLMs like Gemma 3 and Llama 3.2 by grounding reasoning in knowledge graphs via two specialized tools: extract_facts and get_hint, leveraging a Relational Graph Convolutional Network (RGCN). Evaluated on the CLUTRR kinship benchmark in both Oracle (ground truth) and Realistic (self-extracted knowledge) settings, RGCN hints improved performance by 1.5–2× over text-only baselines. The approach is limited by fact extraction bottlenecks and error propagation in sequential deduction. Additionally, a distraction effect was observed where noisy self-generated facts sometimes reduced performance despite expert hints. This work outlines challenges in symbolic grounding for low-resource neuro-symbolic agents and suggests iterative verification strategies.
why_it_matters_zh: 本文针对SLMs推理能力不足的问题，通过结合符号知识和神经网络方法，显著提升了其多跳逻辑推理性能，推动了低资源语言模型在实际复杂推理场景中的应用潜力。
why_it_matters_en: By integrating symbolic knowledge extraction and expert neuro-symbolic reasoning, this work substantially improves multi-hop logical reasoning in SLMs, advancing their applicability in complex tasks within resource-constrained environments.

title_zh: 利用多智能体AI与MCP服务器协调电力网研究
title_en: Orchestrating Power Grid Studies with Multi-Agent AI and MCP Servers
summary_zh: 本文探讨了智能体AI与模型上下文协议（MCP）如何支持输电系统运营商（TSO）的电力网研究。重点是将大型语言模型与数值仿真工具、结构化工作流程和人工监督相结合。文章提出了工业界对智能体辅助电网研究的关键需求，介绍了基于MCP的接口pypowsybl-mcp，该接口向AI智能体开放仿真工具pypowsybl的部分功能。该平台可用于研究智能体如何设置仿真、执行分析、检索结果，并通过标准化工具调用与电力系统仿真器交互。文中还讨论了以人为中心的多智能体工作流程原则及结合技术指标和从业者反馈的评估策略，将MCP工具集成视为迈向更交互化、可审计及可扩展电网研究环境的步骤。
summary_en: This paper explores how Agentic AI and the Model Context Protocol (MCP) can aid power-grid studies within a Transmission System Operator (TSO) framework. It focuses on integrating Large Language Models with numerical simulation tools, structured workflows, and human oversight. Key industrial requirements for agent-assisted grid studies are identified, and pypowsybl-mcp—a MCP-based interface exposing select capabilities of the simulation tool pypowsybl to AI agents—is presented. This serves as a testbed for agents to setup simulations, run analyses, fetch results, and interact with power-system simulators via standardized tool calls. The paper also discusses human-in-the-loop multi-agent workflow principles and outlines an evaluation approach combining technical metrics with practitioner feedback, positioning MCP-based tool integration as a step toward more interactive, auditable, and scalable grid-study environments.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着电力系统复杂性提升，传统仿真工具单一操作效率受限。通过多智能体AI与MCP标准接口结合，可以实现更加智能化、灵活且可扩展的仿真研究环境，提升电网运营商的决策支持能力。
why_it_matters_en: As power systems grow more complex, traditional simulation tools operated in isolation face efficiency limits. Combining multi-agent AI with standardized MCP interfaces enables more intelligent, flexible, and scalable simulation environments, enhancing decision support for grid operators.
